issue_summary: Fix NRT reopen (the logic is still horrible, but thats what current trunk does!)
javadoc_before:
/** A FilterIndexReader contains another IndexReader, which it * uses as its basic source of data, possibly transforming the data along the * way or providing additional functionality. The class * FilterIndexReader itself simply implements all abstract methods * of IndexReader with versions that pass all requests to the * contained index reader. Subclasses of FilterIndexReader may * further override some of these methods and may also provide additional * methods and fields. *

Note: The default implementation of {@link FilterIndexReader#doOpenIfChanged} * throws {@link UnsupportedOperationException} (like the base class), * so it's not possible to reopen a FilterIndexReader. * To reopen, you have to first reopen the underlying reader * and wrap it again with the custom filter. */
javadoc_after: /** A FilterIndexReader contains another IndexReader, which it * uses as its basic source of data, possibly transforming the data along the * way or providing additional functionality. The class * FilterIndexReader itself simply implements all abstract methods * of IndexReader with versions that pass all requests to the * contained index reader. Subclasses of FilterIndexReader may * further override some of these methods and may also provide additional * methods and fields. */
